response:
Sec. 1  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their immune system reacts to peanut proteins by releasing histamines which can cause severe allergic reactions such as anaphylaxis which can be life threatening regardless of the environment they are in  
living underwater does not eliminate the presence of peanut proteins which could be introduced through food sources or environmental exposure which would still trigger an allergic response in sensitive individuals  

Sec. 2  
the physical act of submerging in water does not provide immunity against allergens which are chemical in nature and not affected by water or pressure changes  
therefore individuals with peanut allergies face the same risk of allergic reactions underwater as they do on land making it impossible for them to live underwater without proper medical intervention and strict avoidance of allergens
